perturbation.original_text: 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS and HGF may renew the Agreement within two (2) years of the expiry of the initial term upon mutual understanding. 8.2 It is understood that a subsequent renewal of a five (5) year term will be negotiated in good faith and shall carry terms very close to the original Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS and HGF may renew the Agreement within two (2) years of the expiry of the initial term upon mutual understanding. 8.2 It is understood that a subsequent renewal of a ten (10) year term will be negotiated in good faith and shall carry terms very close to the original Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the renewal term from 'five (5) years' to 'ten (10) years'. This contradicts the expectation set in section 8.1 that renewals would maintain a term similar to the initial five-year agreement. This creates uncertainty about the duration of the renewed agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 The initial term of this Agreement shall, unless sooner terminated by consent of all parties, expires in five (5) years from the date of Effective Date. NVOS and HGF may renew the Agreement within two (2) years of the expiry of the initial term upon mutual understanding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal term from 'five (5) years' to 'ten (10) years'. This directly contradicts the initial agreement outlined in section 8.1, which states that the initial term is five years. By altering the renewal term to ten years, it creates an inconsistency regarding the duration of the renewed agreement, directly conflicting with the original agreement's implicit expectation of renewals similar in length to the initial term.

perturbation.original_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of thirty percent (30%) of net Company income basis on an annual basis commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.3 To remunerate HGF on the basis of twenty percent (20%) of net Company income basis on an annual basis commencing 12 months after the first full 12-month revenue period.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the percentage of net company income HGF is entitled to, from thirty percent (30%) to twenty percent (20%). This in-text contradiction directly affects Article 12.1 where the profit splits are clearly defined, creating in-text contradiction, which now leads to ambiguity regarding the correct profit distribution ratio and the amount of money to which each party is entitled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.1 The distribution will be based on NVOS audited review and will be made within three months of annual considerations on the basis of a seventy percent (70%) of net profit to NVOS and thirty percent (30%) of net profit to HGF.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the percentage allocated to HGF from 30% to 20% in Section 9.3. This directly contradicts the pre-existing allocation outlined in Section 12.1, which states that HGF receives 30% of the net profit. This discrepancy creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the correct profit distribution ratio, directly conflicting with the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 The distribution will be based on NVOS audited review and will be made within three months of annual considerations on the basis of a seventy percent (70%) of net profit to NVOS and thirty percent (30%) of net profit to HGF. 12.2 The distribution will be based on NVOS audited review and will be made within three months of annual considerations.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 The distribution will be based on NVOS audited review and will be made within three months of annual considerations on the basis of a seventy percent (70%) of net profit to NVOS and thirty percent (30%) of net profit to HGF. 12.2 The distribution will be based on NVOS unaudited review and will be made within six months of annual considerations.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by modifying the review basis from 'NVOS audited review' to 'NVOS unaudited review' and the time frame for distribution from 'within three months of annual considerations' to 'within six months of annual considerations'. This change directly in-text contradicts Article 15.2 by creating uncertainty over the auditing requirements. It furthermore creates in-text contradiction about the time frame distributions, leading to in-text contradiction which in turn affects payment timeframe, and it is no longer certain when the distributions must be made. This may lead to delays and legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.2 The Company's audited annual filing will be prepared in accordance to NVOS requirements for the purposes of consolidation on a US GAAP accounting basis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the review basis from 'NVOS audited review' to 'NVOS unaudited review' and the timeframe for distribution from 'within three months of annual considerations' to 'within six months of annual considerations'. The contradicted text, 15.2, states 'The Company's audited annual filing will be prepared in accordance to NVOS requirements'. The change from audited to unaudited directly contradicts the requirement of an audited annual filing. This falls under the definition of inconsistencies, as different sections provide conflicting requirements. It introduces ambiguity over the auditing requirements (definition 1 Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction).
